data.title: AM Best Withdraws Credit Ratings of Arabia Insurance Company – Jordan
data.description:
AM Best Withdraws Credit Ratings of Arabia Insurance Company – Jordan

AM Best has maintained the under review with developing implications status for the Financial Strength Rating of B (Fair) and the Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating of “bb” (Fair) of Arabia Insurance Company – Jordan (AICJ) (Jordan). Concurrently, AM Best has withdrawn these Credit Ratings (ratings).

The ratings have been withdrawn further to the sale of AICJ to Jerusalem Insurance Company PLC, which was completed on the 14 September 2025. As a consequence, AM Best’s contractual relationship with AICJ has ended.

The under review with developing implications status reflects uncertainty over AICJ’s future strategy and its financial strength.

This press release relates to Credit Ratings that have been published on AM Best’s website. For all rating information relating to the release and pertinent disclosures, including details of the office responsible for issuing each of the individual ratings referenced in this release, please see AM Best’s Recent Rating Activity web page. For additional information regarding the use and limitations of Credit Rating opinions, please view Guide to Best’s Credit Ratings. For information on the proper use of Best’s Credit Ratings, Best’s Performance Assessments, Best’s Preliminary Credit Assessments and AM Best press releases, please view Guide to Proper Use of Best’s Ratings & Assessments.

AM Best is a global credit rating agency, news publisher and data analytics provider specialising in the insurance industry. Headquartered in the United States, the company does business in over 100 countries with regional offices in London, Amsterdam, Dubai, Hong Kong, Singapore and Mexico City. For more information, visit www.ambest.com.

data.title: Here's how Nike's stock could benefit big from the next World Cup
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi

The company sponsors six top FIFA teams and could see a $1.3 billion revenue boost as it takes advantage of World Cup visibility, an analyst says

RBC said it's more bullish on Nike's revenue prospects due to an expected boost from the FIFA World Cup next year.

Nike Inc. has a big marketing budget and sponsors six of the likely top 10 national teams in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. That opportunity may result in a $1.3 billion revenue boost as the sportswear giant works to realize a turnaround, according to an RBC Capital Markets analyst.

With next year's World Cup taking place on Nike's home turf in North America, the company could put $4.9 billion in marketing muscle behind its brands as it sees a chance to reach a larger audience than the previous FIFA World Cup, which took place in Qatar in 2022, according to RBC Capital Markets analyst Piral Dadhania.

Nike sponsors six top-tier national teams in Brazil, Portugal, France, England, Croatia and the Netherlands, which could help the company gain brand recognition and show off product innovations such as new performance fabrics, he said.

By contrast, Nike's closest competitor, Adidas (XE:ADS) (ADDYY), has a smaller marketing budget of about $3 billion and is likely to sponsor just four of the best teams - Argentina, Spain, Belgium and Germany, per RBC.

"We would expect Nike to utilize its spend advantage at a time when it is revamping its product offer and needs to increase product awareness," Dadhania wrote.

RBC upgraded Nike's stock to outperform from sector-perform, while hiking its price target to $90 from $76. That new target implies the potential for a roughly 25% boost in Nike's stock price over the next 12 months.

The firm now expects Nike to generate 2026 revenue of $47.3 billion, up from its earlier view of $46.5 billion and well above the FactSet consensus estimate of $45.91 billion.

Nike's product pipeline "is already looking stronger" in running and basketball, with Dadhania also bullish on improvements in its apparel lineup starting in this coming January, followed by its 2026 World Cup product launch in March.

Looking ahead, the sporting-goods business is offering Nike a "more favorable environment" for it to stage a comeback in performance categories such as running and training, he noted.

Nike reports its fiscal first-quarter earnings on Sept. 30, with analysts currently expecting the company to generate adjusted profit of 27 cents a share and revenue of $10.98 billion, according to FactSet estimates.

On the company's fiscal fourth-quarter update on June 26, Nike Chief Executive Elliott J. Hill said early feedback on its FIFA product pipeline had been positive, with an uptick in holiday orders from retailers.

"We're finding a better balance with our portfolio of sport performance and new dimensions of sportswear expected to offset the declines in our classic franchise," Hill said, according to a transcript. "We're executing our plans and trending in the right direction but a full recovery will take time."

Nike's stock rose factionally on Thursday and is down by 4.5% in 2025, while the S&P 500 SPX has risen 12.8%. The company's stock has lost about 37% over the past five years as the S&P 500 has doubled.

-Steve Gelsi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  09-18-25 1226ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: A government shutdown is looming. Here's what history says could happen to stocks.
data.description:
By Robert Schroeder

The S&P 500 has largely done better, not worse, in shutdowns

A partial government shutdown is looming on Oct. 1 if lawmakers don't pass a stopgap budget. What has happened historically to U.S. stocks during shutdowns?

Parts of the federal government will shut down on Oct. 1 if Congress can't pass a funding deal before then. What would that mean for the stock market?

If past is prologue, stocks have actually risen in most cases as Washington has deadlocked over spending, taxes or border-security issues. The latest fight is over the exclusion of healthcare money sought by Democrats, and prediction markets see a 56% chance of a shutdown as of Thursday.

While a shutdown would furlough federal government workers, potentially shutter some national parks and possibly disrupt air travel, the stock market might actually reward rather than punish investors, based on past experience.

Now read: What happens to Social Security and other services if the government shuts down in October?

Here's how stocks have handled government shutdowns since 1978.

The above table compiled by Stifel's chief Washington policy strategist, Brian Gardner, shows six government shutdowns since 1978 that lasted five or more trading days, with the S&P 500 index SPX rising in the four most recent closures.

House Republicans are planning to vote by Friday on legislation to avert a shutdown. It would fund the government through Nov. 21 and also includes extra money for lawmakers' security in the wake of the killing of conservative activist Charlie Kirk last week.

The measure excludes an extension of expanded Affordable Care Act subsidies, and Democrats have threatened to withhold votes over the exclusion. Republican leaders have been open to the idea of keeping the enhanced subsidies but say they need more time to iron out details, according to the Wall Street Journal.

Tobin Marcus of Wolfe Research - who also notes markets' "historical tendency to shrug [shutdowns] off" - suggested negotiations over the subsidy issue could continue next month.

"If Democrats fold on their demand for subsidy extension this month, we wouldn't view that as a signal that it's never going to happen, with our [Capitol] Hill sources indicating that they are open to some negotiation process to work out an ACA deal later in the year," he wrote in a note on Tuesday.

-Robert Schroeder

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  09-18-25 1217ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Vinci Gets $2.09 Billion Electrification Contract on Rail Baltica Project
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar 




   Vinci won an electrification contract valued at 1.77 billion euros ($2.09 billion) on the Rail Baltica project in Estonia, Lithuania and Latvia. 



   The French construction and infrastructure company on Thursday said a consortium encompassing its subsidiary Cobra IS and Elecnor has been awarded the Rail Baltica project, covering 870 kilometers of rail in the three Baltic countries. The contract share of Cobra is valued at 885 million euros. 



  "This is the largest rail electrification project currently under way in Europe and will link the Baltic railway system to the rest of Europe," Vinci added. 



  The project will unfold in two phases, the first of which will start on Oct. 1 and be completed in 2030. 





  Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  September 18, 2025 12:19 ET (16:19 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: BLACK DESERT RESORT ANNOUNCES COMPLETION OF ITS RESORT CENTER
data.description:
BLACK DESERT RESORT ANNOUNCES COMPLETION OF ITS RESORT CENTER

BLACK DESERT RESORT ANNOUNCES COMPLETION OF ITS RESORT CENTER

PR Newswire

IVINS, Utah, Sept. 18, 2025

Utah's largest destination resort brings luxury accommodations, a spa and wellness center, diverse dining, and access to outdoor adventure to the region

IVINS, Utah, Sept. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Black Desert Resort, a premier destination for luxury hospitality, golf, and adventure, announced today the completion of its resort center. This expansive hub in Southern Utah sets a new standard of luxury with 447 rooms and suites spanning two distinct offerings—a Hotel and Resort Collection. Guests can also enjoy seven food and beverage outlets, the 15,000-square-foot Plume Spa & Wellness, an infinity-edge pool with cabanas, a 19-hole Tom Weiskopf-designed championship golf course, a wide range of resort activities, and 45,000 square feet of state-of-the-art meeting and event space. Black Desert Resort is masterfully designed to harmonize with the centuries-old canvas of striking black lava beds and burnt-red sandstone cliffs of Greater Zion.

"Our vision was to create luxury without pretense—an experience rooted in the soul of this incredible landscape," said Patrick Manning, Managing Partner of Black Desert Resort. "We didn't want to replicate traditional Southwest motifs with the resort development. Instead, we chose to let the raw beauty of Greater Zion inform every design choice, creating spaces that feel both sophisticated and organically connected to the land. The Resort Center is the heart of that vision, a place for guests to connect, relax, and be inspired."

RESORT PROGRAMMING AND OUTDOOR ADVENTURE
 Black Desert Resort is anchored by a 19-hole Tom Weiskopf-designed championship golf course, which hosts annual events on the PGA TOUR and LPGA Tour. The resort features an infinity-edge pool complete with 12 private cabanas, fire pits, and a large hot tub. Guests enjoy guided hikes led by an adventure concierge, daily fitness classes at Plume Spa & Wellness, culinary workshops with the diverse food and beverage team, golf clinics, and more.

The resort spans over 600 acres of scenic desert landscape and guests receive easy access to nearby landmarks such as Snow Canyon and Zion National Park, as well as 200+ acres of preserved land, six miles of hiking trails, scenic bike paths, and more. Black Desert Resort is in close proximity to Tuacahn Center for the Arts, a stunning red rock amphitheater hosting Broadway-style shows and concerts, and The Kayenta Art Village, a growing enclave of galleries, studios, festivals, and local restaurants in a lively hub that celebrates creativity, connection, and community spirit.

DESIGN & ACCOMMODATIONS
 Scottsdale-based Allen & Philp Architects conceived the overall design of the Resort Center while the architectural concept was led by the Madrid-based firm GVI, which drew direct inspiration from the surrounding ancient lava flows and sandstone mountains to create a contemporary desert aesthetic. Inside, the design was spearheaded by Allen & Philp, Patrick Manning and Molly Enos, Director of Design for Reef Capital Partners. Guests can discover a palette of rich basalt, warm wood tones, and glowing orange glass accents that evoke desert sunsets. Expansive floor-to-ceiling windows blur the line between the indoors and the breathtaking vistas of the golf course and surrounding landscape. The trio wanted the interiors to feel like a seamless extension of the stunning natural scenery. By layering organic textures, locally quarried stone, and mid-century modern sensibilities, the duo crafted a sense of rugged elegance that can be seen from the custom millwork to bespoke lighting that mimics the desert night sky. Every detail is designed to immerse guests in a refined yet authentic atmosphere.

The newly completed Resort Center at Black Desert introduces a differentiated hospitality experience through three distinct accommodations: the Hotel Collection, Resort Collection, and Plume Wellness Collection—each thoughtfully designed to meet the needs of today's modern traveler.

  • The Hotel Collection is where modern luxury meets intuitive comfort. Located at the heart of the resort, these rooms offer seamless access to dining, spa, fitness, pool, and convention spaces—ideal for both business travelers and spontaneous desert getaways. Guests can expect sleek furnishings, plush bedding, and high-speed connectivity, wrapped in refined simplicity.
  • The Resort Collection brings casual luxury and comfort together just steps from the resort's vibrant core. These well-appointed suites are ideal for families, groups, or longer stays, offering residential-style layouts with sofa beds, kitchenettes, and easy access to all main resort amenities.
  • The Plume Wellness Collection offers a sanctuary of stillness, healing, and harmony near the Plume Spa & Wellness Center. With only eight thoughtfully appointed rooms, this collection caters to wellness-minded guests with amenities such as circadian lighting, CBD-infused pillows, infrared therapy blankets, yoga and mindfulness tools, and aromatherapy using locally harvested botanicals. It's a space where nature's rhythm supports intentional rest and rejuvenation.

Across all three collections, guests enjoy elevated amenities including a welcome beverage upon arrival, furnished balconies, artwork inspired by Greater Zion, a personalized pillow menu by Malouf™ where guests can choose their ideal pillow type for a restful, customized sleep experience, on-demand golf cart transportation, exclusive access to the putting and driving range, and daily use of the pool, hot tub, and state-of-the-art fitness center. 

With the Hotel Collection's 148 rooms and suites, the Resort Collection's 299 rooms and suites, and the soon-to-open Terrace Collection adding another 174, Black Desert Resort continues to expand its footprint as a premier destination with 600+ luxury accommodations designed for every type of traveler.

CULINARY
 The culinary program at Black Desert Resort stands as a destination in its own right, presenting a portfolio of seven unique dining concepts. Among these are its signature restaurants: Basalt, an elevated American steakhouse showcasing prime cuts and local purveyors, and Latitude, a casual, open-air eatery specializing in wood-fired beef, seafood, and vegetable-forward cuisine. Additional culinary offerings include an indoor-outdoor lobby bar for inventive cocktails and small plates, an artisan coffee and dessert bar with gelato and pastries, a rooftop lounge offering 270-degree views and live entertainment, a lively sports bar with hearty bites local craft beer, simulated golf bays and a vibrant poolside bar for fresh salads and frozen beverages.

SPA & WELLNESS
 Plume Spa & Wellness serves as the cornerstone of the resort's wellness program. The 15,000-square-foot spa and fitness center signals a new era of high-end longevity and spa experiences in Southern Utah and is designed to offer guests a profound journey towards renewal, emotional balance, mental clarity, and physical rejuvenation. Plume Spa & Wellness offers 13 state-of-the-art treatment rooms, a robust treatment menu that focuses on healing and restorative therapies and treatments, as well as a Longevity Lounge featuring high-tech equipment and immersive experiences for guests, including a Dry Cryo Float, Hyperbaric Chamber, Salt Inhalation and more. For those seeking a deeper retreat, the dedicated Plume Wellness Collection offers nine guest accommodations centered around wellbeing, providing an exclusive haven for profound relaxation and rediscovery.

MEETINGS & EVENTS
 Black Desert Resort offers more than 45,000 square feet of versatile indoor and outdoor space with stunning views of the vermillion sandstone cliffs and striking black lava beds. Featuring 18 distinct venues such as a 10,000-square-foot ballroom, five breakout rooms, naturally lit spaces, and expansive event lawns capable of hosting up to 500 guests. The resort offers a dedicated events and conference team, along with an on-site chef-driven banquet and catering team, and is equipped to host conventions, conferences, trade shows, weddings, and various special events.

SUSTAINABILITY
 Black Desert Resort is deeply committed to ecosystem preservation. 67 percent of the 600 acres surrounding the resort are protected open space, and the team has incorporated sustainable practices into the fabric of both the construction and daily operations. Black Desert Resort broke the record for C-Pace funding and is the world's largest to use low-voltage Power over Ethernet (POE) for energy efficiency and employs an advanced water-reuse system.

In partnership with the Utah Division of Wildlife Resources and Utah Tech University, Black Desert Resort established a protected lake habitat for the endangered Virgin River chub. This fish species is native to the Virgin River and is threatened due to habitat loss and drought. In an effort to protect this species, 400 Virgin River chubs have been released into one of the lakes, providing a safe haven for these fish to grow and reproduce, away from predators and other environmental stressors.

Black Desert Resort is elevating the guest experience with local collaborations—featuring UT47 Certified Utah Beef across dining venues, Redmond Real Salt on the table, and Hydralyte wellness packets in-room. Together, these partnerships highlight the best of Utah craftsmanship and care.

Starting rates for Black Desert Resort begin at $399/night. Future developments of Black Desert Resort will include a water park, pickleball courts, additional rooms and suites, and more dining, shopping, and entertainment options. For more information, please visit blackdesertresort.com or call 844-237-8824. Follow @blackdesertresort on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn.

Media Assets
 Resort Center images can be found here.

About Black Desert Resort
 Set amidst the vermillion sandstone cliffs and striking black lava beds of Greater Zion, Black Desert Resort is a premier destination for luxury hospitality, golf, and Southern Utah adventure. Black Desert Resort is anchored by a Tom Weiskopf-designed championship golf course, and hosts annual events on the PGA TOUR and LPGA Tour. The Resort Center includes 447 rooms and suites, seven dining venues, a 15,000-square-foot spa, state-of-the-art pickleball courts, a heated outdoor pool and hot tub, and 45,000 square feet of indoor/outdoor meeting and event space. Future phases will include a water park, more rooms and suites, and many more dining, shopping, and entertainment options. Spanning over 600 acres of scenic landscape, Black Desert Resort offers easy access to 200+ acres of preserved land and six miles of hiking trails.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/black-desert-resort-announces-completion-of-its-resort-center-302559795.html

SOURCE Black Desert Resort

data.title: VinFast VF 9: An EV That Gives Big Families Everything They Asked For
data.description:
VinFast VF 9: An EV That Gives Big Families Everything They Asked For

As Canadian families look for electric vehicles that can handle hockey bags, long commutes, and life as a “third space,” the VF 9 stands out as one of the few three-row options offering both space and range in a market still short on choice.

Picture a September evening in Burlington, Ontario. The first hints of fall drift through the air as a parent loads a hockey bag into the back of a three-row electric SUV. The cabin is already cooled to a comfortable 22 degrees while a double-double coffee rests in the cupholder. Canadians know the value of cabin room. Multipurpose vehicles, including SUVs and crossovers, accounted for 63.5 percent of new registrations in the first quarter of 2025, according to Statistics Canada, while passenger cars made up only 11.9 percent. Yet families arriving at dealerships often find the aisle for truly spacious EVs narrower than demand suggests.

A Shrinking Aisle for Spacious EVs

The past two years have brought both excitement and retreat in the market for large electric vehicles. Several high-profile three-row projects announced in 2023 and 2024 have since been delayed or shelved, thinning showroom variety. For Canadians seeking a seven-seat electric option, that leaves only a handful of choices.

The appetite for space is obvious. The hockey bags and camping gear that fill garages across the country explain why multipurpose vehicles continue to dominate registrations, while sedans lag far behind, as the numbers above have shown.

The return of commuting reinforces the need. By May 2024, the average drive to work in Canada reached 26.4 minutes, nearly identical to pre-pandemic levels1. Long daily trips require comfort and utility, not only for drivers but for everyone sharing the ride.

That shift is paired with a cultural change in how Canadians view their cars. A 2024 survey by DTS/Xperi2 found that 65 percent of Gen Z drivers and 63 percent of millennials already see their vehicle as a “third space,” something between home and office. Sixty-one percent expect that feeling to deepen soon. In practical terms, the car becomes a hub for work calls, entertainment, and downtime.

This growing demand for multipurpose EVs meets a market still finding its footing. Small electric crossovers are now common, but three-row options remain limited. For households considering a switch from gasoline, the choice set is expanding, though not yet as wide as many might hope.

The VF 9: Room to Grow in Canada

Against that backdrop, the seven-passenger VF 9 arrived in Canada in the final months of 2024 and has been steadily finding its place in the nation’s driveways. Both trims come standard with dual motors, 123-kWh batteries, and all-wheel drive. The setup delivers 402 horsepower and, in the Eco version, an EPA-rated range of up to 518 kilometers. On a fast charger, the battery moves from 10 to 70 percent in about 35 minutes, a pace that fits neatly into a rest stop meal.

Inside, the VF 9 leans into the idea of a “third space.” Optional captain’s chairs recline like business-class seats. A panoramic roof fills the cabin with light. Heated and massaging seats ease winter fatigue on longer trips. Rear screens in the Plus trim stream shows and movies, a feature many families say they want. In a mid-2024 survey, 58 percent of respondents said they would like a rear screen in their next car.3

Pricing starts at 77,310 Canadian dollars for the Eco trim and 81,310 for the Plus. Coverage includes a 10-year or 200,000-kilometer vehicle warranty, along with unlimited-kilometer battery protection for the same period. For many buyers, that assurance helps address questions about long-term reliability.

That long-term reliability, along with the VF 9’s space and range, will be crucial as Canada and other nations work toward a net-zero future. Larger households will need EVs that deliver comfort without compromise, and vehicles like the VF 9 show that family haulers can still be big, but quieter.

____________________________

1 https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/n1/pub/11-627-m/11-627-m2025009-eng.htm

2 https://www.advanced-television.com/2024/12/05/report-consumers-embracing-cars-as-a-third-space/

3 https://www.jalopnik.com/people-apparently-want-more-screens-inside-their-cars-1851564698/

 

John Lindo – john.lindo@vinfastauto.com

data.title: Velocity Flexible Workspace Named “Best in Coworking & Shared Space” in Westchester Magazine’s Best of Business Awards
data.description:
Velocity Flexible Workspace Named “Best in Coworking & Shared Space” in Westchester Magazine’s Best of Business Awards

Velocity Flexible Workspace is proud to announce that it has been named Best in Coworking & Shared Space by Westchester Magazine’s prestigious Best of Business Awards 2025.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250918946249/en/

Located in the heart of Westchester County, Velocity Flexible Workspace offers a modern, professional environment designed for today’s businesses. With flexible office options, affordable all-inclusive pricing, and fully serviced amenities, the workspace is tailored to support entrepreneurs, small businesses, and professionals seeking convenience and reliability.

Professional Offices with Flexible, All-Inclusive Solutions

Velocity Flexible Workspace eliminates the hassle of traditional office leasing by providing turnkey solutions. All office options include essential services such as high-speed internet, utilities, cleaning, and access to professional meeting rooms—ensuring businesses can focus on growth instead of overhead.

Velocity Flexible Workspace offers:
-Private offices designed for productivity
-Flexible coworking options for independent professionals
-Conference and meeting rooms ready when you need them
-All-inclusive pricing with no hidden costs

Recognition for Excellence in Westchester County

The award underscores Velocity Flexible Workspace’s role as a trusted provider of professional, affordable, and flexible offices with services in Westchester County.

“Our mission is to make office space simple, professional, and cost-effective,” said Mark Blandford, Executive Vice President of Diamond Properties. “We’re honored to be recognized as the best in coworking and shared space, and we look forward to continuing to provide businesses in Westchester with the flexibility and value they deserve.”

About Velocity Flexible Workspace

Velocity Flexible Workspace is part of Diamond Properties’ diverse portfolio, which includes more than 125 properties nationwide across commercial real estate, hospitality, and entertainment. Located in downtown Mount Kisco, Velocity Flexible Workspace offers affordable, flexible, and fully serviced office solutions for businesses at every stage.

Learn More

Discover why Velocity Flexible Workspace was voted Best in Coworking & Shared Space in Westchester County.

Visit www.velocityflexibleworkspace.com to learn more or schedule a tour.
